header.headline: Operator Spectrum Map
header.subtitle: Position operator families on one continuum instead of forcing a false binary.

left.title: Hand-Tuned Kernels
left.body: Operators are highly specialized, tightly controlled, and usually shaped around a narrow runtime contract.

right.title: Generic Operator Families
right.body: Operators are more flexible, compositional, and easier to route across broader workloads.

continuum.label: Specialized <-> Composable
continuum.note: The useful lesson is not one winner, but where each operator family belongs on the line.

band_1.title: Registry Anchors
band_1.body: Stable named operators tie the left side to deterministic runtime paths.

band_2.title: Mixed Middle
band_2.body: Many useful operators blend fixed kernels with configurable structure.

band_3.title: Composable Surface
band_3.body: The right side favors broader semantic composition over hand-tuned specialization.

footer.note: Spectrum maps help the model reason about structured continua rather than only discrete family switches.
